blocks.content: Six months after the Ferris wheel.

The room is small and warm — a side room off the chapel, borrowed mirrors and borrowed light, the particular organized chaos of a wedding morning. Someone has brought coffee that nobody has touched. Ethan's jacket is on a hanger on the back of the door and the rest of him is almost assembled, which is where Nathan comes in.
Nathan steps behind him at the mirror and picks up the tie.
He pulls it loose and starts over with the calm efficiency of a man who has watched too many tutorials about this and would like the credit.

Ethan watches this in the mirror. He looks like himself — the same face, the same quiet quality — but different in the way things that have been decided differently. He has the particular stillness of someone who has been moving toward something for a long time and has finally arrived at the threshold of it.
Nathan looks at him in the mirror for a moment. Then he nods once and keeps working on the tie.
